categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: Starpower IGBT modules feature advanced trench field-stop technology with voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V. These modules offer high power density, low switching losses, and excellent thermal performance for industrial drives, EVs, and renewable energy applications.
categories.longDescription: Starpower's IGBT module portfolio represents the company's core competency in high-power semiconductor packaging. The modules utilize advanced trench field-stop IGBT chips combined with optimized freewheeling diodes to deliver industry-leading performance. Voltage ratings span from 600V for industrial drives to 1700V for high-power renewable energy and traction applications. Current ratings range from tens to hundreds of amperes, accommodating applications from small servo drives to large MW-scale inverters. Starpower's proprietary packaging technology ensures excellent thermal management, low parasitic inductance, and high reliability under demanding operating conditions. All modules undergo comprehensive testing including static parameters, switching characteristics, and thermal cycling. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides technical support for module selection, thermal design, and application optimization. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBT Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: 600V-650V IGBT Modules
series.description: Low-voltage IGBT modules optimized for industrial motor drives and power supplies

series.name: 1200V IGBT Modules
series.description: Medium-voltage IGBT modules for EVs, solar inverters, and industrial drives

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Starpower IGBT Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right IGBT module based on voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and thermal requirements

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for Starpower IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower IGBT modules are available in three main voltage ratings: (1) 600V-650V series for industrial motor drives, servo systems, and power supplies with input voltages up to 400VAC. (2) 1200V series for electric vehicle powertrains, solar inverters, and 690VAC industrial drives. (3) 1700V series for high-voltage applications including wind power converters, traction drives, and medium-voltage drives. The voltage rating should be selected based on the DC bus voltage with adequate margin (typically 1.5-2x) for voltage transients and switching spikes. Starpower provides detailed voltage derating curves for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What packages are available for Starpower IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower IGBT modules are available in industry-standard packages: (1) 34mm and 62mm half-bridge modules - standard industrial packages compatible with major manufacturers. (2) EasyPIM and EasyPACK - compact modules with integrated rectifier and brake chopper. (3) EconoDUAL and EconoPACK - high-power modules for industrial drives and renewable energy. (4) Custom packages - specialized modules for automotive and traction applications. All packages feature low-inductance design, direct bonded copper (DBC) substrates, and optimized thermal interfaces. Package selection depends on power rating, cooling method, and mechanical mounting requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package recommendations based on your thermal and mechanical requirements.

faqs.question: How do Starpower IGBT modules compare to international brands?
faqs.answer: Starpower IGBT modules offer competitive performance compared to international brands: (1) Electrical performance - comparable VCE(sat), switching losses, and SOA characteristics. (2) Thermal performance - advanced DBC substrates and thermal interface materials provide excellent heat dissipation. (3) Reliability - extensive qualification testing including temperature cycling, power cycling, and HTRB. (4) Cost - typically 20-30% more cost-effective than equivalent international brands. (5) Supply - shorter lead times and better availability for Asia-Pacific customers. Starpower modules have been qualified by major industrial and automotive OEMs, proving field reliability in millions of installations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request a detailed comparison report or samples to evaluate Starpower modules for your application.

products.shortDescription: 1200V 50A IGBT module with 1.7V VCE(sat), ideal for industrial motor drives and power supplies.
products.description: GD50HFL120C1S is a 1200V 50A half-bridge IGBT module featuring trench field-stop technology. With low VCE(sat) of 1.7V and optimized switching characteristics, it delivers high efficiency in industrial motor drives, servo systems, and power conversion applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 50A half-bridge IGBT module with trench field-stop technology. Low 1.7V VCE(sat) for reduced conduction losses.
34mm standard package with excellent thermal performance. Optimized for switching frequencies up to 20kHz.
In stock for industrial drive and power supply applications. FAE support for thermal design and gate drive optimization.
features: Trench field-stop technology with 1.7V VCE(sat) typical

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GD50HFL120C1S?
faqs.answer: GD50HFL120C1S is optimized for +15V turn-on and -8V turn-off gate drive: (1) +15V VGE(on) ensures low VCE(sat) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) -8V negative turn-off voltage prevents false turn-on due to dv/dt and Miller effect. (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 15-33Ω for turn-on and 10-22Ω for turn-off. The gate drive should provide 2-4A peak current for fast switching. Starpower provides recommended gate drive circuits and PCB layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GD50HFL120C1S?
faqs.answer: GD50HFL120C1S in 34mm package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.65K/W (IGBT) and 1.2K/W (diode). (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +125°C. For continuous operation at 50A with 1.7V VCE(sat), the conduction loss is 85W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.5K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient. Thermal interface material with low thermal resistance (0.1-0.2K·cm²/W) is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GD50HFL120C1S be used for servo drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GD50HFL120C1S is well-suited for servo drive applications. Its characteristics make it ideal for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating provides adequate margin for 380-480VAC servo systems. (2) 50A current rating supports servo drives up to 15-20kW. (3) Fast switching with controlled dv/dt reduces motor insulation stress. (4) Low VCE(sat) minimizes losses during frequent acceleration/deceleration cycles. (5) 10μs short-circuit withstand provides reliable protection during output faults. The module's compact 34mm package fits standard servo drive mechanical designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for servo drive design support and PWM frequency optimization.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand time of GD50HFL120C1S?
faqs.answer: GD50HFL120C1S has a short-circuit withstand time (tSC) of 10μs minimum at VGE = 15V, VCC = 800V, and starting Tj = 125°C. This specification ensures reliable protection during output short-circuit conditions. The IGBT desaturation detection circuit should detect the fault and turn off the IGBT within 10μs to prevent device damage. Starpower recommends using gate drivers with integrated desaturation protection for reliable short-circuit protection. The module has been tested for repetitive short-circuit robustness under specified conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for short-circuit protection circuit design and desaturation detection implementation.

faqs.question: What is the diode recovery characteristics of the built-in anti-parallel diode?
faqs.answer: GD50HFL120C1S's built-in anti-parallel diode features: (1) Fast reverse recovery time trr = 120ns typical at IF = 25A. (2) Soft recovery characteristics to minimize voltage overshoot and EMI. (3) Low forward voltage VF = 1.8V typical at IF = 25A. (4) Reverse recovery charge Qrr = 1.2μC typical. These characteristics make the diode suitable for high-frequency freewheeling applications in inverter bridges. The soft recovery minimizes dv/dt stress on the complementary IGBT and reduces EMI filtering requirements. The diode is rated for the same current as the IGBT for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode recovery characteristics data and snubber circuit design.

faqs.question: Is GD50HFL120C1S suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: GD50HFL120C1S is built on an industrial-grade platform. For automotive applications, Starpower offers AEC-Q101 qualified versions with enhanced reliability testing. The automotive versions undergo additional testing including: (1) Temperature cycling (-40°C to +150°C). (2) High-temperature operating life (HTOL) at 150°C. (3) Power cycling for package reliability. (4) ESD testing per AEC-Q101. For automotive applications such as EV auxiliary drives, HVAC compressors, and DC-DC converters, the automotive-qualified versions are recommended. Contact Starpower for automotive qualification reports and PPAP documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive-qualified module recommendations and qualification data.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

62mm standard package with excellent thermal performance. Optimized for switching frequencies up to 15kHz.
In stock for high-power industrial and renewable energy applications. FAE support for thermal design and system optimization.
features: Trench field-stop technology with 1.75V VCE(sat) typical

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GD100HFL120C2S?
faqs.answer: GD100HFL120C2S is optimized for +15V turn-on and -8V turn-off gate drive: (1) +15V VGE(on) ensures low VCE(sat) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) -8V negative turn-off voltage prevents false turn-on due to dv/dt and Miller effect. (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 10-22Ω for turn-on and 8-15Ω for turn-off. The gate drive should provide 4-6A peak current for fast switching of the larger die size. Starpower provides recommended gate drive circuits and PCB layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GD100HFL120C2S?
faqs.answer: GD100HFL120C2S in 62mm package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.35K/W (IGBT) and 0.65K/W (diode). (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +125°C. For continuous operation at 100A with 1.75V VCE(sat), the conduction loss is 175W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.2K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient. Forced air cooling or liquid cooling is typically required for this power level.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and cooling system design.

faqs.question: Can GD100HFL120C2S be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GD100HFL120C2S is well-suited for solar inverter applications. Its characteristics make it ideal for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating supports 480V AC three-phase inverters and 1000V DC string inverters. (2) 100A current rating supports inverters up to 50-75kW. (3) Optimized switching losses enable 16kHz operation for compact filter design. (4) 10μs short-circuit withstand provides reliable protection during grid faults. (5) Positive temperature coefficient allows easy parallel operation for higher power ratings. The 62mm package is widely used in solar inverter designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for solar inverter design support and MPPT optimization.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand time of GD100HFL120C2S?
faqs.answer: GD100HFL120C2S has a short-circuit withstand time (tSC) of 10μs minimum at VGE = 15V, VCC = 800V, and starting Tj = 125°C. This specification ensures reliable protection during output short-circuit conditions. The IGBT desaturation detection circuit should detect the fault and turn off the IGBT within 10μs to prevent device damage. Starpower recommends using gate drivers with integrated desaturation protection for reliable short-circuit protection. The module's larger die size provides robust short-circuit capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for short-circuit protection circuit design and desaturation detection implementation.

faqs.question: What is the diode recovery characteristics of the built-in anti-parallel diode?
faqs.answer: GD100HFL120C2S's built-in anti-parallel diode features: (1) Fast reverse recovery time trr = 150ns typical at IF = 50A. (2) Soft recovery characteristics to minimize voltage overshoot and EMI. (3) Low forward voltage VF = 1.85V typical at IF = 50A. (4) Reverse recovery charge Qrr = 2.5μC typical. These characteristics make the diode suitable for high-frequency freewheeling applications in inverter bridges. The soft recovery minimizes dv/dt stress on the complementary IGBT and reduces EMI filtering requirements. The diode is rated for the same current as the IGBT for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode recovery characteristics data and snubber circuit design.

faqs.question: How does GD100HFL120C2S compare to GD50HFL120C1S?
faqs.answer: GD100HFL120C2S and GD50HFL120C1S serve different power levels in 1200V applications: (1) GD100HFL120C2S (100A, 62mm) is designed for higher power applications (30-75kW) with lower RthJC (0.35K/W vs 0.65K/W). (2) GD50HFL120C1S (50A, 34mm) is suitable for lower power applications (15-30kW) with more compact package. Both feature similar VCE(sat) (~1.7V) and switching characteristics. The 62mm package has better thermal performance but requires larger heatsink. For parallel operation, GD100HFL120C2S is preferred due to better current sharing characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for IGBT module selection based on your specific power and thermal requirements.

categories.name: SiC Modules
categories.description: Starpower SiC modules feature silicon carbide MOSFETs and Schottky diodes for high-frequency, high-efficiency power conversion. These modules enable system-level benefits including reduced cooling requirements, smaller passive components, and higher power density.
categories.longDescription: Starpower's SiC module portfolio represents the cutting edge of power semiconductor technology. Silicon carbide (SiC) offers superior material properties compared to silicon, including higher breakdown voltage, lower on-resistance, and faster switching speeds. Starpower's SiC modules combine these benefits with robust packaging technology to deliver unmatched performance in demanding applications. The modules feature SiC MOSFETs with ultra-low RDS(on) and SiC Schottky diodes with zero reverse recovery, enabling switching frequencies up to 100kHz. This allows for dramatic reductions in passive component size, filter weight, and cooling system requirements. Applications include EV charging infrastructure, solar inverters, and high-frequency power supplies where efficiency and power density are critical. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides technical support for SiC module selection, gate driver design, and application optimization. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: 1200V SiC Modules
series.description: High-voltage SiC modules for EV charging and renewable energy

series.name: 650V SiC Modules
series.description: Low-voltage SiC modules for industrial and automotive applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Starpower SiC Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right SiC module based on voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and efficiency requirements

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Starpower SiC modules over IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower SiC modules offer significant advantages over IGBT modules: (1) Higher switching frequency - SiC can switch at 50-100kHz vs 10-20kHz for IGBT, enabling smaller filters and magnetics. (2) Lower switching losses - SiC has negligible switching losses, improving efficiency especially at light loads. (3) Lower conduction losses - SiC MOSFETs have lower RDS(on) temperature coefficient. (4) No tail current - SiC MOSFETs have no tail current during turn-off, reducing switching losses. (5) Higher efficiency - typically 1-3% higher system efficiency. (6) Reduced cooling - lower losses mean smaller heatsinks and cooling systems. The trade-off is higher cost, but system-level savings often justify the premium.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC vs IGBT comparison and application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements do Starpower SiC modules have?
faqs.answer: Starpower SiC modules have specific gate drive requirements: (1) Gate voltage - typically +15V to +18V for turn-on and -3V to -5V for turn-off. The negative turn-off voltage is important to prevent false turn-on due to high dv/dt. (2) Gate resistance - lower than IGBT, typically 5-15Ω, to achieve fast switching. (3) Gate driver capability - 5-10A peak current for fast charging/discharging of gate capacitance. (4) Isolation - reinforced isolation required due to high dv/dt (up to 100V/ns). (5) Protection - active Miller clamp recommended for high dv/dt immunity. Starpower provides recommended gate driver ICs and reference circuits for their SiC modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver recommendations and circuit design guidelines.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from Starpower SiC modules?
faqs.answer: Applications that benefit most from Starpower SiC modules include: (1) EV charging - high-frequency operation enables compact designs for DC fast chargers. (2) Solar inverters - higher switching frequency reduces filter size and weight. (3) High-frequency power supplies - enables MHz-level switching for ultra-compact designs. (4) Automotive DC-DC converters - high efficiency and power density for onboard converters. (5) Industrial drives - higher switching frequency reduces motor current ripple and audible noise. (6) Energy storage systems - bidirectional operation with high efficiency. The key criteria are applications where switching frequency, efficiency, or power density is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for application suitability assessment and ROI analysis.

products.shortDescription: 1200V 100A SiC MOSFET module with 12mΩ RDS(on), ideal for EV charging and solar inverters.
products.description: GDS100M12B1 is a 1200V 100A SiC MOSFET module featuring silicon carbide technology. With ultra-low RDS(on) of 12mΩ and fast switching capability, it enables high-efficiency, high-frequency power conversion for demanding applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 100A SiC MOSFET module with 12mΩ RDS(on). Silicon carbide technology for ultra-fast switching.
Easy1B package with integrated NTC and low parasitic inductance. Supports switching up to 100kHz.
In stock for EV charging and renewable energy applications. FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.
features: Silicon carbide MOSFET with 12mΩ RDS(on) at 25°C
Zero reverse recovery charge with SiC Schottky diode
Low switching losses for high efficiency

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GDS100M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS100M12B1 is optimized for +18V turn-on and -3V to -5V turn-off gate drive: (1) +18V VGS(on) ensures low RDS(on) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) Negative turn-off voltage (-3V to -5V) is critical to prevent false turn-on due to high dv/dt (>50V/ns). (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 5-10Ω for both turn-on and turn-off. (4) The gate drive should provide 5-10A peak current for fast switching. (5) Active Miller clamp is recommended for high dv/dt immunity. Starpower provides detailed gate drive recommendations and reference circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC gate driver IC recommendations and circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDS100M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS100M12B1 in Easy1B package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.4K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +150°C. For continuous operation at 100A with 12mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 120W at 150°C (higher than 25°C due to positive temperature coefficient). This requires a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.3K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 150°C at 40°C ambient. High-performance thermal interface material is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GDS100M12B1 be used for EV fast charging applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDS100M12B1 is ideal for EV fast charging applications. Its characteristics make it perfect for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating supports 800V battery systems and 1000V DC bus. (2) 100A current rating supports DC fast chargers up to 100-150kW. (3) 100kHz switching enables compact magnetic components. (4) High efficiency (>98%) reduces cooling requirements. (5) Bidirectional capability supports V2G applications. (6) High reliability for 24/7 operation. The Easy1B package is widely used in EV charger designs and compatible with standard heatsinks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV charger design support and topology recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the switching performance of GDS100M12B1 compared to IGBT?
faqs.answer: GDS100M12B1 offers dramatically superior switching performance compared to IGBT: (1) Switching frequency - up to 100kHz vs 10-20kHz for IGBT. (2) Turn-on time - typically 50ns vs 200-500ns for IGBT. (3) Turn-off time - typically 30ns vs 300-800ns for IGBT (including tail). (4) Switching losses - approximately 1/10th of equivalent IGBT. (5) dv/dt - up to 100V/ns vs 5-10V/ns for IGBT. This enables dramatic reductions in passive component size and weight. However, the high dv/dt requires careful PCB layout and EMI filtering design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching performance analysis and EMI mitigation strategies.

faqs.question: What are the layout considerations for GDS100M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS100M12B1 requires careful PCB layout due to high switching speed: (1) Minimize gate loop inductance - use wide, short traces and place gate driver close to module. (2) Kelvin source connection - use separate source connection for gate drive to avoid source inductance feedback. (3) Decoupling capacitors - place high-frequency ceramic capacitors (100nF-1μF) very close to module terminals. (4) Minimize power loop inductance - use laminated busbars or parallel planes for DC link. (5) EMI considerations - implement proper shielding and filtering due to high dv/dt. (6) Thermal vias - use sufficient thermal vias under the module for heat dissipation. Starpower provides detailed layout guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for PCB layout review and optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: How does the cost of GDS100M12B1 compare to IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: GDS100M12B1 has higher component cost than equivalent IGBT modules (typically 2-3x), but system-level cost may be lower: (1) Passive components - smaller filters and magnetics due to high frequency (30-50% savings). (2) Cooling - smaller heatsinks and fans due to higher efficiency (20-30% savings). (3) Enclosure - smaller size and weight reduce enclosure cost. (4) Energy savings - 1-3% higher efficiency reduces operating costs. (5) Reliability - lower operating temperatures improve system reliability. For high-volume applications, the total cost of ownership often favors SiC. Starpower can provide ROI analysis for specific applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for system cost analysis and ROI calculations.

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GDS50M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS50M12B1 is optimized for +18V turn-on and -3V to -5V turn-off gate drive: (1) +18V VGS(on) ensures low RDS(on) and minimizes conduction losses. (2) Negative turn-off voltage (-3V to -5V) is critical to prevent false turn-on due to high dv/dt. (3) Gate resistor selection affects switching speed and EMI - typical values are 8-15Ω for both turn-on and turn-off. (4) The gate drive should provide 3-6A peak current for fast switching. (5) Active Miller clamp is recommended for high dv/dt immunity. The smaller die size requires less gate charge than GDS100M12B1.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC gate driver IC recommendations and circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDS50M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS50M12B1 in Easy1B package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.6K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +150°C. For continuous operation at 50A with 25mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 62.5W at 150°C. This requires a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.5K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 150°C at 40°C ambient. Natural convection cooling may be sufficient for lower power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and cooling system design.

faqs.question: Can GDS50M12B1 be used for automotive DC-DC converter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDS50M12B1 is well-suited for automotive DC-DC converter applications. Its characteristics make it ideal for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating supports 800V EV platforms and provides margin for load dump. (2) 50A current rating supports DC-DC converters up to 10-15kW. (3) 100kHz switching enables compact magnetics for onboard converters. (4) High efficiency reduces cooling requirements in space-constrained automotive environment. (5) AEC-Q101 qualified versions available for automotive applications. (6) Compact Easy1B package fits automotive form factors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive DC-DC design support and AEC-Q101 qualification data.

faqs.question: What is the difference between GDS50M12B1 and GDS100M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS50M12B1 and GDS100M12B1 serve different current levels: (1) GDS50M12B1 (50A, 25mΩ) is designed for lower current applications (10-25kW) with higher RDS(on) but lower cost. (2) GDS100M12B1 (100A, 12mΩ) is for higher current applications (25-50kW) with lower RDS(on) and better thermal performance. Both feature the same Easy1B package and 1200V rating. GDS50M12B1 requires less gate charge (lower gate driver power) and may be sufficient for many applications. For parallel operation, GDS100M12B1 is preferred due to better current sharing. Selection depends on current requirements and cost optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for SiC module selection based on your specific current and efficiency requirements.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit capability of GDS50M12B1?
faqs.answer: GDS50M12B1 has limited short-circuit withstand capability compared to IGBT: (1) Short-circuit withstand time is typically 2-5μs vs 10μs for IGBT. (2) This is due to higher current density and faster thermal runaway in SiC. (3) Fast desaturation detection (<2μs) is critical for protection. (4) Soft turn-off is recommended to avoid overvoltage spikes. (5) Current limiting during startup and fault conditions is important. Starpower provides recommended protection circuits and desaturation detection guidelines. The fast switching of SiC actually helps in quickly limiting fault current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for short-circuit protection design and desaturation detection circuits.

faqs.question: What are the EMI considerations for GDS50M12B1 designs?
faqs.answer: GDS50M12B1 requires careful EMI management due to high dv/dt: (1) dv/dt can exceed 50V/ns, generating high-frequency EMI. (2) Common mode currents through parasitic capacitances can cause EMI issues. (3) Proper shielding and grounding are essential. (4) EMI filters need to be designed for higher frequency content. (5) Snubber circuits may be needed to reduce voltage overshoot. (6) PCB layout should minimize loop areas and use proper grounding. Starpower provides EMI testing data and mitigation recommendations. The benefits of SiC often outweigh the EMI challenges with proper design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EMI analysis and filter design support.

categories.name: MOSFET Modules
categories.description: Starpower MOSFET modules feature high-current MOSFETs in robust packages for switching power applications. These modules offer fast switching, low on-resistance, and high reliability for industrial and automotive power electronics.
categories.longDescription: Starpower's MOSFET module portfolio provides high-current switching solutions for power electronics applications. The modules utilize advanced trench MOSFET technology with ultra-low RDS(on) and fast switching characteristics. Voltage ratings range from 60V to 200V for low-voltage high-current applications such as DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power distribution systems. Current ratings extend to hundreds of amperes to meet demanding application requirements. Starpower's proprietary packaging technology ensures excellent thermal performance, low parasitic inductance, and high reliability. All modules undergo comprehensive testing including static parameters, switching characteristics, and reliability verification. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides technical support for MOSFET module selection, gate drive design, and thermal management. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive MOSFET Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: 60V-100V MOSFET Modules
series.description: Low-voltage high-current MOSFET modules for DC-DC and motor applications

series.name: 150V-200V MOSFET Modules
series.description: Medium-voltage MOSFET modules for industrial power electronics

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Starpower MOSFET Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right MOSFET module based on voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and thermal requirements

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Starpower MOSFET modules over discrete MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Starpower MOSFET modules offer several advantages over discrete MOSFETs: (1) Higher current capability - modules can handle hundreds of amperes vs tens of amperes for discrete devices. (2) Better thermal performance - modules use DBC substrates and advanced thermal interface materials for efficient heat dissipation. (3) Lower parasitic inductance - integrated design reduces stray inductance for cleaner switching. (4) Easier assembly - modules simplify mechanical mounting and electrical connections. (5) Higher reliability - qualified modules undergo extensive testing for industrial applications. (6) Compact design - modules achieve higher power density than discrete solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for module vs discrete selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for Starpower MOSFET modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower MOSFET modules are available in voltage ratings from 60V to 200V: (1) 60V-100V series for low-voltage high-current applications such as 48V battery systems, DC-DC converters, and low-voltage motor drives. (2) 150V-200V series for medium-voltage applications including 110V/220V industrial drives and power supplies. The voltage rating should be selected with adequate margin (typically 1.3-1.5x) for voltage transients and switching spikes. Lower voltage ratings offer lower RDS(on) for the same die size, optimizing conduction losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What applications are best suited for Starpower MOSFET modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower MOSFET modules are ideal for high-current switching applications: (1) DC-DC converters - high-frequency buck/boost converters for battery systems and power supplies. (2) Motor drives - low-voltage high-current motor control for EVs and industrial applications. (3) Power distribution - solid-state switches and circuit breakers for high-current distribution. (4) Battery management - high-current battery switches and protection circuits. (5) Welding equipment - high-current switching for welding power supplies. (6) Induction heating - high-frequency switching for heating applications. The key criteria are high current (>50A) and low voltage (<200V).
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for application suitability assessment and module recommendations.

products.shortDescription: 60V 200A MOSFET module with 1.8mΩ RDS(on), ideal for high-current DC-DC and motor applications.
products.description: GDM200N60 is a 60V 200A MOSFET module featuring advanced trench technology. With ultra-low RDS(on) of 1.8mΩ and fast switching capability, it delivers high efficiency in high-current DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power distribution applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 60V 200A MOSFET module with 1.8mΩ RDS(on). Advanced trench technology for high-efficiency switching.
EasyPACK package with excellent thermal performance and low parasitic inductance. Supports switching up to 100kHz.
In stock for high-current power applications. FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.
features: Advanced trench MOSFET with 1.8mΩ RDS(on) at 25°C
Low gate charge Qg = 180nC for fast switching
High current capability: 200A continuous at 25°C case
Low parasitic inductance for clean switching

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GDM200N60?
faqs.answer: GDM200N60 is optimized for 10V gate drive (VGS = 10V) where it achieves the specified 1.8mΩ RDS(on). The MOSFET can also operate with 4.5V gate drive for compatibility with logic-level drivers, though RDS(on) increases to approximately 2.8mΩ. The gate threshold voltage VGS(th) is typically 2.5V. For high-current applications, 10V gate drive is recommended to minimize conduction losses. The gate drive circuit should provide fast rise/fall times (typically 20-50ns) to minimize switching losses. Gate resistor values of 2-5Ω are typical for this module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDM200N60?
faqs.answer: GDM200N60 in EasyPACK package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.5K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +150°C. For continuous operation at 200A with 1.8mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 72W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.4K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient. Forced air cooling is typically required for continuous operation at full current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GDM200N60 be used for 48V battery management systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDM200N60 is ideal for 48V battery management systems. Its characteristics make it perfect for this application: (1) 60V rating provides adequate margin for 48V nominal systems (max 60V during charging). (2) 200A current rating supports high-current battery switches and protection circuits. (3) Ultra-low RDS(on) of 1.8mΩ minimizes conduction losses and heating. (4) Fast switching enables active balancing circuits. (5) EasyPACK package provides reliable mounting and thermal management. (6) Low gate charge allows fast switching for protection functions. The module is widely used in 48V mild hybrid and energy storage systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for BMS design support and protection circuit recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the switching performance of GDM200N60?
faqs.answer: GDM200N60 offers excellent switching performance for high-current applications: (1) Turn-on time tr = 25ns typical with proper gate drive. (2) Turn-off time tf = 15ns typical. (3) Gate charge Qg = 180nC at VGS = 10V. (4) Input capacitance Ciss = 12nF typical. (5) Output capacitance Coss = 2.5nF typical. (6) Reverse transfer capacitance Crss = 0.8nF typical. These characteristics enable switching frequencies up to 100kHz with reasonable switching losses. The low parasitic inductance of the EasyPACK package minimizes voltage overshoot during switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching performance analysis and optimization.

faqs.question: How does GDM200N60 compare to discrete MOSFETs for parallel operation?
faqs.answer: GDM200N60 offers advantages over paralleled discrete MOSFETs: (1) Better current sharing - integrated design ensures balanced current distribution. (2) Lower parasitic inductance - module package has lower stray inductance than PCB traces between discrete devices. (3) Simplified thermal management - single heatsink interface vs multiple devices. (4) Easier gate drive - single gate connection vs multiple parallel gates. (5) Higher reliability - qualified module vs discrete component assembly. (6) Compact footprint - module achieves higher power density. For currents above 100A, modules are generally preferred over discrete paralleling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for module vs discrete comparison for your specific application.

faqs.question: What is the avalanche capability of GDM200N60?
faqs.answer: GDM200N60 has specified avalanche capability for rugged operation: (1) Single-pulse avalanche energy EAS = 800mJ at starting Tj = 25°C. (2) Repetitive avalanche energy EAR = 50mJ at 25°C. (3) Avalanche current IAR = 100A. This ensures the module can safely absorb energy from unclamped inductive switching transients. However, for applications with frequent inductive switching, external protection such as TVS diodes or RC snubbers is recommended to limit avalanche stress and improve long-term reliability. The module is 100% avalanche tested during production.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for avalanche ruggedness testing data and inductive load protection design.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

products.shortDescription: 80V 150A MOSFET module with 3.2mΩ RDS(on), ideal for industrial DC-DC and motor applications.
products.description: GDM100N80 is an 80V 150A MOSFET module featuring advanced trench technology. With low RDS(on) of 3.2mΩ and fast switching capability, it delivers high efficiency in industrial DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power distribution applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 80V 150A MOSFET module with 3.2mΩ RDS(on). Advanced trench technology for high-efficiency switching.
EasyPACK package with excellent thermal performance and low parasitic inductance. Supports switching up to 100kHz.
In stock for industrial power applications. FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.
features: Advanced trench MOSFET with 3.2mΩ RDS(on) at 25°C
Low gate charge Qg = 120nC for fast switching
High current capability: 150A continuous at 25°C case
Low parasitic inductance for clean switching

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage for GDM100N80?
faqs.answer: GDM100N80 is optimized for 10V gate drive (VGS = 10V) where it achieves the specified 3.2mΩ RDS(on). The MOSFET can also operate with 4.5V gate drive for compatibility with logic-level drivers, though RDS(on) increases to approximately 5mΩ. The gate threshold voltage VGS(th) is typically 2.2V. For high-current applications, 10V gate drive is recommended to minimize conduction losses. The gate drive circuit should provide fast rise/fall times to minimize switching losses. Gate resistor values of 3-6Ω are typical for this module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate driver IC recommendations and gate drive circuit design.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDM100N80?
faqs.answer: GDM100N80 in EasyPACK package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.6K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +150°C. For continuous operation at 150A with 3.2mΩ RDS(on), the conduction loss is 72W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.5K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient. Forced air cooling is recommended for continuous operation at full current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GDM100N80 be used for 72V EV auxiliary systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDM100N80 is well-suited for 72V EV auxiliary systems. Its characteristics make it ideal for this application: (1) 80V rating provides adequate margin for 72V nominal systems with voltage transients. (2) 150A current rating supports high-power auxiliary drives such as HVAC compressors and power steering. (3) Low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses for battery efficiency. (4) Fast switching allows PWM frequencies up to 20kHz for quiet operation. (5) Rugged EasyPACK package withstands automotive vibration and temperature cycling. (6) AEC-Q101 qualified versions available for automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV auxiliary system design support and automotive qualification data.

faqs.question: What is the switching performance of GDM100N80?
faqs.answer: GDM100N80 offers excellent switching performance: (1) Turn-on time tr = 20ns typical with proper gate drive. (2) Turn-off time tf = 12ns typical. (3) Gate charge Qg = 120nC at VGS = 10V. (4) Input capacitance Ciss = 8nF typical. (5) Output capacitance Coss = 1.8nF typical. (6) Reverse transfer capacitance Crss = 0.6nF typical. These characteristics enable switching frequencies up to 100kHz. The lower gate charge compared to GDM200N60 allows faster switching with less gate driver power, making it suitable for high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for switching performance analysis and frequency optimization.

faqs.question: How does GDM100N80 compare to GDM200N60?
faqs.answer: GDM100N80 and GDM200N60 serve different voltage and current requirements: (1) GDM100N80 (80V, 150A, 3.2mΩ) is designed for higher voltage applications (60-72V) with moderate current. (2) GDM200N60 (60V, 200A, 1.8mΩ) is for lower voltage (48V) with higher current. GDM100N80 has higher voltage rating but lower current capability. GDM200N60 offers lower RDS(on) for the same die size due to lower voltage rating. Selection depends on system voltage and current requirements. Both use the same EasyPACK package and have similar thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for MOSFET module selection based on your voltage and current requirements.

faqs.question: What protection features are recommended for GDM100N80 applications?
faqs.answer: Recommended protection features for GDM100N80 applications: (1) Overcurrent protection - current sensing with fast shutdown (<1μs) for short-circuit protection. (2) Over-temperature protection - NTC thermistor monitoring with shutdown at 125-150°C. (3) Overvoltage protection - TVS diodes or active clamping for voltage transients. (4) Undervoltage lockout - prevent operation with insufficient gate drive voltage. (5) Shoot-through protection - dead-time control and cross-conduction prevention in bridge configurations. (6) Soft-start - current limiting during startup to prevent inrush current. Starpower provides recommended protection circuit designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for protection circuit design and component recommendations.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE

categories.name: Rectifier Modules
categories.description: Starpower rectifier modules feature high-power diodes and thyristors for AC-DC power conversion. These modules offer high current capability, low forward voltage drop, and excellent thermal performance for industrial rectification applications.
categories.longDescription: Starpower's rectifier module portfolio provides reliable AC-DC power conversion solutions for demanding industrial applications. The modules utilize high-power diodes and phase-control thyristors (SCRs) with low forward voltage drop and high surge current capability. Current ratings range from hundreds to thousands of amperes, accommodating applications from small welding machines to large plating power supplies. Voltage ratings span from 800V to 6000V for various input voltage requirements. Starpower's proprietary packaging technology ensures excellent thermal management, low thermal resistance, and long-term reliability under harsh operating conditions. All modules undergo comprehensive testing including forward characteristics, reverse recovery, and thermal cycling. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides technical support for rectifier module selection, thermal design, and application optimization. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Rectifier Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: Diode Rectifier Modules
series.description: High-power diode modules for uncontrolled rectification applications

series.name: Thyristor Modules
series.description: Phase-control thyristor modules for controlled rectification

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Starpower Rectifier Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right rectifier module based on voltage rating, current capacity, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between diode and thyristor rectifier modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower offers both diode and thyristor rectifier modules: (1) Diode modules - uncontrolled rectification with fixed output voltage determined by input AC voltage. Simple, reliable, and cost-effective for applications not requiring voltage control. (2) Thyristor (SCR) modules - phase-controlled rectification allowing output voltage adjustment by controlling firing angle. Used for soft starters, DC motor speed control, and power regulation. Thyristors can handle high surge currents and are robust for industrial applications. Selection depends on whether voltage control is required. Both types offer high current capability and low forward voltage drop.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode vs thyristor selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for Starpower rectifier modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower rectifier modules are available in a wide range of voltage ratings: (1) 800V-1200V for standard industrial applications with 380-480VAC input. (2) 1600V-2200V for medium-voltage applications and higher safety margins. (3) 3000V-6000V for high-voltage applications such as traction drives and power transmission. The voltage rating should be selected based on the peak reverse voltage (PRV) requirement with adequate margin (typically 2-3x) for voltage transients and line surges. Higher voltage ratings provide better reliability but may have slightly higher forward voltage drop.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What applications are best suited for Starpower rectifier modules?
faqs.answer: Starpower rectifier modules are ideal for high-current AC-DC power conversion: (1) Welding equipment - high-current rectification for arc welding power supplies. (2) Electroplating - controlled DC power for plating baths requiring high current at low voltage. (3) DC motor drives - rectification and control for DC motor speed regulation. (4) Battery chargers - high-current charging for industrial battery systems. (5) Induction heating - rectification for high-frequency heating power supplies. (6) Power supplies - AC-DC conversion for industrial DC power distribution. The key criteria are high current (>100A) and need for reliable, efficient rectification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for application suitability assessment and rectifier recommendations.

products.shortDescription: 1200V 200A diode rectifier module with 0.85V VF, ideal for welding and plating applications.
products.description: GDR200A12 is a 1200V 200A diode rectifier module featuring low forward voltage drop and high surge current capability. With VF of 0.85V and excellent thermal performance, it delivers reliable rectification for welding machines, plating power supplies, and industrial DC power applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 200A diode rectifier module with 0.85V forward voltage. High surge current capability for reliable operation.
34mm standard package with excellent thermal performance. Low thermal resistance for high-current applications.
In stock for welding and plating applications. FAE support for thermal design and system optimization.
features: Low forward voltage drop: 0.85V at IF = 200A

faqs.question: What is the forward voltage drop of GDR200A12?
faqs.answer: GDR200A12 has a low forward voltage drop of 0.85V typical at rated current (IF = 200A) and 25°C junction temperature. The forward voltage has a positive temperature coefficient, increasing to approximately 0.75V at 125°C. This low VF minimizes conduction losses, improving overall rectifier efficiency. For a 200A rectifier, the conduction loss is 170W per diode at rated current. The low VF is achieved through advanced chip design and optimization of the conducting area. Starpower provides detailed forward characteristics curves in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for forward voltage analysis and efficiency calculations.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDR200A12?
faqs.answer: GDR200A12 in 34mm package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.45K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +125°C. For continuous operation at 200A with 0.85V VF, the conduction loss is 170W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.3K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 125°C at 40°C ambient. Forced air cooling is typically required for continuous operation at full current. Proper thermal interface material is essential for good heat transfer.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GDR200A12 be used for welding machine applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDR200A12 is ideal for welding machine applications. Its characteristics make it perfect for this demanding application: (1) 1200V rating provides adequate margin for 380-480VAC input rectification. (2) 200A current rating supports welding power supplies up to 50-75kW. (3) Low VF of 0.85V minimizes conduction losses and heating. (4) High surge current capability (2000A) withstands inrush currents during arc starting. (5) Fast reverse recovery (200ns) reduces switching losses in high-frequency welding. (6) Rugged 34mm package withstands vibration and thermal cycling in welding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for welding power supply design support and rectifier configuration.

faqs.question: What is the reverse recovery time of GDR200A12?
faqs.answer: GDR200A12 has a fast reverse recovery time of 200ns typical at IF = 200A and di/dt = 200A/μs. The reverse recovery charge Qrr is approximately 40μC. Fast reverse recovery is important for high-frequency rectification and reduces switching losses in applications with rapid current commutation. The soft recovery characteristics minimize voltage overshoot and EMI. For standard 50/60Hz rectification, reverse recovery is not critical, but for high-frequency applications such as welding inverters, fast recovery reduces losses and improves efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for reverse recovery characteristics data and high-frequency rectification design.

faqs.question: How does GDR200A12 compare to thyristor modules for rectification?
faqs.answer: GDR200A12 (diode) and thyristor modules serve different rectification needs: (1) GDR200A12 provides uncontrolled rectification with fixed output voltage determined by AC input. Simple and reliable but no voltage control. (2) Thyristor modules provide phase-controlled rectification allowing output voltage adjustment. More complex control but enables soft-start and voltage regulation. Diodes have lower forward voltage drop and are more efficient for simple rectification. Thyristors are needed when voltage control or soft-start is required. For welding applications, diode bridges are often preferred for simplicity, while thyristors are used when current control is needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for diode vs thyristor selection for your specific rectification requirements.

faqs.question: What is the surge current capability of GDR200A12?
faqs.answer: GDR200A12 has high surge current capability for reliable operation under overload conditions: (1) Non-repetitive surge current IFSM = 2000A for 10ms half-sine wave. (2) Repetitive surge current capability depends on duty cycle and cooling. (3) I²t rating = 20,000 A²s for fuse coordination. This high surge capability ensures the module can withstand inrush currents during startup, capacitor charging, and fault conditions. For applications with frequent high-current transients, proper heatsinking and thermal management are essential to prevent junction temperature from exceeding limits during surge events.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for surge current analysis and protection coordination.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Good performance and reliability. Suitable for various industrial applications.

products.shortDescription: 1600V 100A thyristor module with 1.05V VF, ideal for soft starters and motor control.
products.description: GDT100A16 is a 1600V 100A phase-control thyristor (SCR) module featuring low forward voltage drop and high dv/dt capability. With VF of 1.05V and excellent triggering characteristics, it delivers reliable phase-controlled rectification for soft starters, DC motor drives, and power control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1600V 100A thyristor module with 1.05V forward voltage. Phase-control capability for voltage regulation.
34mm standard package with excellent thermal performance. High dv/dt capability for reliable operation.
In stock for motor control and power regulation applications. FAE support for firing circuit design.
features: Low forward voltage drop: 1.05V at IT = 100A

faqs.question: What is the gate trigger requirement for GDT100A16?
faqs.answer: GDT100A16 has the following gate trigger characteristics: (1) Gate trigger current IGT = 100mA maximum at VGT = 1.5V. (2) Gate trigger voltage VGT = 1.5V maximum. (3) Gate non-trigger voltage VGD = 0.2V minimum to prevent false triggering. (4) Holding current IH = 100mA typical. The gate drive circuit should provide at least 150mA gate current with fast rise time (<1μs) for reliable triggering. For phase-control applications, the gate pulse should be properly synchronized with the AC line and have adequate width (typically 20-100μs) to ensure latching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate drive circuit design and firing angle control.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of GDT100A16?
faqs.answer: GDT100A16 in 34mm package has the following thermal characteristics: (1) Junction-to-case thermal resistance RthJC = 0.5K/W. (2) Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 125°C (lower than diodes due to thyristor characteristics). (3) Recommended operating junction temperature Tj(op) = -40°C to +100°C. For continuous operation at 100A with 1.05V VF, the conduction loss is 105W, requiring a heatsink with thermal resistance of approximately 0.3K/W or better to maintain junction temperature below 100°C at 40°C ambient. Forced air cooling is typically required for continuous operation at full current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thermal simulation and heatsink selection guidance.

faqs.question: Can GDT100A16 be used for motor soft starter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, GDT100A16 is ideal for motor soft starter applications. Its characteristics make it perfect for this application: (1) 1600V rating provides adequate margin for 380-480VAC motor applications with voltage transients. (2) 100A current rating supports motors up to 50-75kW. (3) Phase-control capability enables smooth voltage ramping during startup. (4) High surge current capability withstands motor inrush currents (5-7x FLA). (5) High dv/dt capability prevents false triggering from motor voltage transients. (6) Rugged 34mm package withstands industrial environment. Soft starters reduce mechanical stress and extend motor life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for soft starter design support and control algorithm recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the dv/dt capability of GDT100A16?
faqs.answer: GDT100A16 has high dv/dt capability for reliable operation in industrial environments: (1) Critical rate of rise of off-state voltage (dv/dt)c = 1000V/μs minimum at rated junction temperature. (2) This high dv/dt capability prevents false triggering from rapid voltage transients. (3) For applications with higher dv/dt, snubber circuits may be required to limit the rate of voltage rise. (4) The high dv/dt rating is achieved through advanced chip design and optimized gate structure. This makes GDT100A16 suitable for applications with high line transients such as motor drives and power controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for dv/dt analysis and snubber circuit design if needed.

faqs.question: How does GDT100A16 compare to GDR200A12 for rectification?
faqs.answer: GDT100A16 (thyristor) and GDR200A12 (diode) serve different rectification needs: (1) GDT100A16 provides phase-controlled rectification with adjustable output voltage (0-100% of maximum). Requires gate control circuit but enables soft-start and voltage regulation. (2) GDR200A12 provides uncontrolled rectification with fixed output voltage. Simpler but no voltage control. GDT100A16 has higher forward voltage (1.05V vs 0.85V) and lower current rating (100A vs 200A). Thyristors are preferred when voltage control is needed; diodes are preferred for simple, high-efficiency rectification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for thyristor vs diode selection based on your control requirements.

faqs.question: What commutation considerations are needed for GDT100A16?
faqs.answer: GDT100A16 requires proper commutation for reliable turn-off in AC applications: (1) The thyristor turns off when anode current falls below holding current (IH = 100mA). (2) A minimum commutation time (turn-off time tq = 100μs) is required before forward voltage can be reapplied. (3) For line-commutated applications (50/60Hz), natural commutation from AC line is sufficient. (4) For inverter applications, forced commutation or load commutation is required. (5) Snubber circuits may be needed to limit reapplied dv/dt during commutation. Starpower provides commutation guidelines for various application topologies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for commutation analysis and circuit design support.
